para_3: You get a raise and suddenly you're spending more. You upgrade your car, your apartment, your lifestyle. You feel like you're finally living better. But your bank account doesn't grow. You're making more but still living paycheck to paycheck. This is lifestyle creep. As income goes up, spending goes up to match it. In the US, we measure success by what we own. Your coworkers have nice cars. Friends post vacation photos. Social media shows constant comparison. But lifestyle creep eats up every extra dollar. You go from barely making it on $50,000 to barely making it on $70,000. More money, same stress. You adjusted your life to require that new income. Higher car payment. Higher rent. More subscriptions. No cushion. No savings growth. The smartest move when you get a raise is to save the difference. Live on your old income and bank the new money. But resisting the urge to upgrade is hard, and most people can't. Lifestyle creep keeps you broke at every income level.
